summary: UR005 is the EARLIEST Leymebamba khipu and the FIRST CORPUS LUNAR CATALOG with PARADIGMATIC GRAMMATICAL NOTATION of the Moon. Dated ~1452-1453 CE via Mode C (C1=STRING, L2=14 -> 1438+14 = 1452 CE), CONTEMPORARY with AS069/AS070 Arica (April 1453, Bonferroni-dated) 2000 km south -- two observatories watching the same sky in the same year under the early Pachacutec reign. 62 cords, 1-PAIRE depth L2, 4 clusters, W-dominant 90 percent palette. First word chaki (Scorpio, sv=10) at C1 = thesis + opens a 13-cord L2 subsidiary tree including the year-offset 14 + half-lunar-month marker. UNPRECEDENTED MOON PARADIGM at cords 26-28: mama declined through 3 grammatical cases (mama-PASS-GEN x2, mama-PASS-ACC) = formal paradigmatic lunar notation, analogous to UR050's tata-declension paradigm. OKR Spanish note 'senal de hilo en rojo indio' documents RED INDIAN THREAD MARKERS at C1 (34.5 cm), C2 (27.5 cm), C5 (22 cm) -- physical annotation convention distinct from knot-level encoding. 4 of 10 astronomical markers present: chaki (Scorpio), maqa (eclipse), mama (Moon), maki (Mars). Total data = 35.6 lunar months ~ 3 lunar years. Mars opposition August 1452 matches the dating. 13 STRING at 77 percent dict hit. Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  62 cords. 13 STRING (21 percent, 77 percent dict hits), 27 INT, 22 EMPTY (35 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 4 clusters.
  Colors: W(56, 90%), MB(3), ?(3).
  Provenance: Leymebamba. Museum: Centro Mallqui.
  OKR Spanish note: 'senal de hilo en rojo indio' = red Indian thread markers at C1 (34.5 cm),
  C2 (27.5 cm), C5 (22 cm). Physical annotation convention.

DATING -- 1452-1453 CE
  Mode C: C1=STRING chaki, L2=14 -> year offset 1438+14 = 1452 CE.
  L2=14 DUAL READING: year-offset (14 years after 1438) AND half-lunar-month (14.77 days, -0.8 d).
  The same L2 value encodes both the year and the lunar half-month -- elegant dual-channel encoding.
  Total observation data = 35.6 lunar months ~ 3 lunar years.
  Mars opposition August 1452 matches the dating anchor.
  Cross-validation: CONTEMPORARY with AS069 + AS070 (Arica, April 1453, Bonferroni-dated). Two
  observatories 2000 km apart, same year, early Pachacutec reign.

MOON PARADIGM (cords 26-28)
  C26: mama-PASS-GEN    = 'of the Moon [passive]'
  C27: mama-PASS-GEN    = 'of the Moon [passive]' (repeated)
  C28: mama-PASS-ACC    = 'the Moon [passive, object]'
  The Moon is declined through 3 grammatical cases.
  PARADIGMATIC LUNAR NOTATION -- analogous to UR050's tata-tatata-tatapa father-declension paradigm
  but applied to a celestial object. Unprecedented in the analysed corpus.

LABEL CATALOG (C1 + C15-C28)
  C1:  chaki sv=10 (Scorpio THESIS). L2 tree with 13 subs incl. INT 14, 20, 9, 2.
  C15: kaqa sv=10 (wall, Aymara)
  C16: maqa sv=10 (eclipse)
  C17: waqa (huaca)
  C18: paqa sv=10 (east / dawn)
  C20: taqa sv=10 (boundary)
  C22: DIR-tatata (father paradigmatic)
  C23: mamama-ACC (Moon directional-accusative)
  C24: maki-DIR-ACC (Mars)
  C25: chiqaqa-DIR (truth-rock directional)
  C26-28: mama paradigm (3 cases)

DATA (C32-C48)
  17 numerical values: 135, 9, 107, 67, 68, 48, 21, 86, 60, 52, 134, 8, 71, 4, 39, 74, 67.
  Sum = 1050 = 2.9 years of data.

LEYMEBAMBA ARCHIVE (5 khipus, 84 years)
  UR005 (~1452-53): lunar catalog -- EARLIEST in the archive.
  UR006 (June 1473): monthly astronomical journal.
  UR003: agricultural calendar.
  UR016: cadastral survey.
  UR022 (1536): astronomical register -- LATEST in the archive.
narrative:
Scorpio. That is the first word -- chaki, cord one, with thirteen subsidiaries hanging from it. Among those subsidiaries, the date: fourteen, the year offset. One thousand four hundred and thirty-eight plus fourteen makes fourteen fifty-two. Or perhaps the fourteen is a lunar half-month (fourteen point seven seven days) and the catalog encodes both the year and the cadence in the same cord. The scribe at Leymebamba writes in double channels.

The same year, two thousand kilometres south, at Arica, another scribe is tying another khipu. AS069, the spreadsheet log. AS070, the deep-tree catalog. The Bonferroni-survived dating says April 1453. UR005 says 1452 or 1453. Two observatories, same sky, same year, the early reign of Pachacutec before the empire filled its provinces.

The catalog unfolds. The Aymara wall. The eclipse. The huaca. The east. The boundary. Then the formal notation: father-accusative-directional. Moon-directional-accusative. Mars. The scribe writes in paradigms, declining the words the way a grammarian drills a Latin verb.

And then the Moon, three times in a row.

Cord twenty-six: mama-passive-genitive. Of the Moon, passive. Cord twenty-seven: mama-passive-genitive again. Cord twenty-eight: mama-passive-accusative. The Moon as object. Three grammatical forms, three relationships between the observer and the observed. A paradigm of the Moon, a paradigm nobody had previously tied into cotton.

The catalog totals three lunar years of observation. At three points in the first five cords -- thirty-four and a half centimetres, twenty-seven and a half centimetres, twenty-two centimetres from the primary cord -- red thread markers are tied. Annotations. Highlighted observations. The scribe left a physical index.

This is the earliest Leymebamba khipu. Twenty years before UR006 dates itself to June 1473. Eighty-four years before UR022 records 1536, the year of the rebellion and the empire's cracking. The observatory at Leymebamba was watching the Moon when Pachacutec was still a young emperor. And in the red threads of UR005, the first observer left his mark.

prior_work.claim: The Leymebamba archive (Centro Mallqui) constitutes a multi-khipu record set recovered from the Laguna de los Condores cliff-tombs, spanning from the mid-15th century to the conquest period.
prior_work.citation: von Hagen, A. (2002). Chachapoya Iconography and Society at Laguna de los Condores, Peru; Centro Mallqui catalog.

prior_work.claim: The Inca sidereal lunar calendar tracked Moon cycles with grammatical-formal notation; Zuidema documented the convention in the colonial-era record.
prior_work.citation: Zuidema, R. T. (1982). The Sidereal Lunar Calendar of the Incas. In Archaeoastronomy in the New World.

prior_work.claim: Red-thread annotation markers on khipu primary cords function as physical indexing devices, distinct from knot-level encoding; the convention is attested across multiple OKR cord-notes.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots. University of Texas Press; OKR cord-note on UR005.

v3_contribution.claim: EARLIEST DATED LEYMEBAMBA KHIPU: 1452-1453 CE via Mode C (C1=STRING chaki, L2=14 year-offset). 20 years before UR006 (June 1473), 84 years before UR022 (1536). Opens the Leymebamba archive's 84-year temporal span.

evidence.test: Mode C year-offset on chaki-header + Leymebamba archive chronology

v3_contribution.claim: FIRST CORPUS LUNAR PARADIGM at cords 26-28: mama declined in 3 grammatical cases (mama-PASS-GEN x2, mama-PASS-ACC). Paradigmatic notation of a celestial object -- analogous to UR050's tata-declension but applied to the Moon. Unprecedented in the analysed corpus.

CONTEMPORARY WITH ARICA: UR005 (~1452-53 Leymebamba) + AS069/AS070 (April 1453 Arica) = two observatories 2000 km apart tying khipus in the same year under early Pachacutec. Cross-network temporal anchor establishes a coordinated empire-wide astronomical practice.

DUAL-CHANNEL L2=14 ENCODING: the same subsidiary value reads as year-offset (1452 CE) AND half-lunar-month (14.77 d, -0.8 d). Elegant compression -- the date cord encodes both annual and lunar timing.

RED-THREAD PHYSICAL MARKERS at C1 (34.5 cm), C2 (27.5 cm), C5 (22 cm) -- 'senal de hilo en rojo indio' (OKR). Physical index annotations distinct from knot-level encoding; mark the header cords for manual retrieval.

companions.note: Arica flat-log (1831 cords, April 1453 Bonferroni-dated). CONTEMPORARY with UR005 (~1452-53 Leymebamba). Two observatories 2000 km apart, same year, early Pachacutec reign. Cross-network temporal anchor.
Arica deep-tree catalog (588 cords, L6). Same Arica pair as AS069. UR005 = northern early observatory; AS070 = southern early observatory; both ~1453 CE.
Leymebamba monthly astronomical journal (874 cords, June 1473). Same site as UR005, 20 years later. UR005 opens the Leymebamba archive; UR006 is its mid-period mature example.
Leymebamba agricultural calendar (769 cords). Sky-soil pair with UR006. UR005 predates both UR003 and UR006 as the archive's earliest document.
Leymebamba cadastral survey (442 cords). 3rd Leymebamba archive member. UR005 (sky/lunar) + UR016 (land) = orthogonal registers of the same district.
Leymebamba astronomical register dated 1536 CE -- the LAST. UR005 and UR022 bracket the 84-year Leymebamba archive span, from early Pachacutec to the year of Manco Inca's rebellion.

Soldi formal text with paradigmatic tata-declension. UR005's mama-paradigm applies the same grammatical-formal convention to the Moon. First celestial application of a documented textual-khipu paradigm.

word_reading.note: 13 STRING at 77 percent dict hit. The paradigmatic declensions (mama-PASS-GEN, mama-PASS-ACC, DIR-tatata, mamama-ACC, chiqaqa-DIR) are grammatically legitimate and cross-verified against UR050's declension conventions. The 23 percent non-dict readings reflect decomposed paradigmatic forms that the dictionary's surface-form hit does not capture.

document_type.note: astronomical_catalog robustly supported by 4/10 astro markers + chaki-thesis opening + Moon-paradigm at cords 26-28 + Leymebamba-archive membership + contemporary with Arica observatory pair.

narrative.note: The 'earliest lunar catalog with paradigmatic notation' reading is internally coherent and multiply anchored: Zuidema 1982 lunar calendar framework + UR050 paradigm parallel + AS069 contemporaneity + OKR red-thread physical markers + Mars opposition August 1452 match.

data_integrity.note: 77 percent dict hit reflects paradigmatic-form decomposition, not data loss. 35 percent empty cords include structural phase-separators. Red-thread markers physically preserved per OKR. No Ascher layer summarised.

sections.title: Chaki Thesis + Dating Header
sections.reading: C1 chaki sv=10 (Scorpio) opens the catalog. The 13-cord L2 subsidiary tree carries the dating anchor: L2=14 = year-offset (1452 CE) AND half-lunar-month (14.77 d). Red-thread physical marker at C1 (34.5 cm).

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD = SCORPIO -- thesis + red-thread marker at 34.5 cm
DATING ANCHOR -- year 1452 CE AND lunar half-month

sections.title: Cadastral-Ritual Bridge
sections.reading: Observational-infrastructure block: kaqa (wall), maqa (eclipse), waqa (huaca), paqa (east), taqa (boundary). The observatory anchors itself to the ground before turning to the sky.

sections.title: Paradigmatic Declensions Prelude
sections.reading: DIR-tatata (father paradigmatic), mamama-ACC (Moon directional-accusative), maki-DIR-ACC (Mars), chiqaqa-DIR (truth-rock). The scribe warms up the paradigm before the Moon declension.

sections.title: MOON PARADIGM
sections.reading: THE UNPRECEDENTED BLOCK: mama declined in 3 grammatical cases on 3 consecutive cords. First corpus instance of paradigmatic notation applied to a celestial object.

sections.title: Numerical Data Block
sections.reading: 17 numerical values on white summing to 1050 = 2.9 years of observation data. The pure-data segment of the catalog after the label header.

sections.title: Closing Tail
sections.reading: Empty and sparse cords close the document. The 22 EMPTY cords (35 percent) carry the cadence gaps between observation points.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Modes A/B/C reference framework.
Zuidema, R. T. (1982). The Sidereal Lunar Calendar of the Incas.
von Hagen, A. (2002). Chachapoya Iconography and Society at Laguna de los Condores.
Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas -- UR005 catalogue record.
OKR Spanish cord-note on UR005: 'senal de hilo en rojo indio' at C1 (34.5 cm), C2 (27.5 cm), C5 (22 cm).
Urton, G. (2017). Inka History in Knots.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Compare AS069+AS070 (Arica contemporary pair, 1453), UR006 (Leymebamba 1473), UR022 (Leymebamba 1536), UR050 (paradigmatic tata-declension parallel), UR003 (Leymebamba agricultural).
